MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Nicola Till, Esq. (415.397.2222; ntill@longlevit.com) of Long & Levit LLP for Plaintiff.
Michael Brook, Esq. (707.889.7189; mbrooklaw@gmail.com) for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DUANE MORRIS LLP's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And The Production Of Documents:

Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses and The Production of Documents is granted as unopposed, with the exception that moving party has conceded that the discovery dispute has been resolved as to Requests for Production Nos. 23, 24, 57, 58, 64, 65, 66, 67, 73, and 77.

Counsel for Plaintill to submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in Word format to jameshfleming@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
